risk_if_not_implemented: Without Automation Support of Availability of Information / Support, AI and autonomous technology risks may go ungoverned, leading to unintended outcomes or exploitation.

compensating_control_1.name: Incident Stakeholder Reporting
compensating_control_1.description:
Mechanisms exist to timely-report incidents to applicable:
(1) Internal stakeholders; 
(2) Affected clients & third-parties; and
(3) Regulatory authorities.
compensating_control_1.justification: Incident Stakeholder Reporting (IRO-10) provides incident response capability that compensates for the absence of Automation Support of Availability of Information / Support (IRO-11.1) by enabling timely detection, containment, and recovery from security events in the absence of the primary control. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Incident Response Plan (IRP)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to maintain and make available a current and viable Incident Response Plan (IRP) to all stakeholders.
compensating_control_2.justification: Incident Response Plan (IRP) (IRO-04) provides incident response capability that compensates for the absence of Automation Support of Availability of Information / Support (IRO-11.1) by enabling timely detection, containment, and recovery from security events in the absence of the primary control. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
